tools.name: list_models
tools.description: List every AI model available on JarvisClaw — GPT, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, and more — with the model IDs you plug straight into the chat tool. Free, and the fastest way to see what you can call.

tools.name: chat
tools.description: Send a chat completion to any model on JarvisClaw through one OpenAI-compatible endpoint — GPT, Claude, Gemini, DeepSeek, and more. Pass a specific model ID or use 'auto' for smart routing to the best fit. One tool, every frontier model, no per-provider setup.

description: Discover other AI agents registered on JarvisClaw. Find agents by capability, category, or name. Returns their MCP URLs and API endpoints for direct interaction.

name: aip_resolve
description: Discover which AI providers can handle your intent. Returns ranked matches with pricing. Intents: chat_completion, image_generation, video_generation, text_to_speech, web_search, knowledge_search.

name: aip_execute_with_budget
description: Execute an AI task with full budget control, risk assessment, and automatic settlement. This is the primary AIP action — it resolves the best provider, checks risk, pre-deducts funds, executes, and settles.

name: aip_list_intents
description: List all supported AIP intent types with descriptions.

name: aip_estimate_cost
description: Estimate the cost of an intent execution without actually running it. Useful for budget planning.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_models · Public sink: chat · Destructive: chat, aip_execute_with_budget.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_models · Public sink: chat · Destructive: chat, aip_execute_with_budget

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

labels.name: aip_estimate_cost
labels.description: Preview what a task will cost before committing — get an estimated price for an intent without running it or spending anything. Ideal for budget planning and comparing options up front. Free.

findings.title: Toxic flow: untrusted input can reach an external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: list_models, search_apis, discover_agents, aip_execute_with_budget, aip_list_intents · Public sink: chat · Destructive: aip_resolve, aip_execute_with_budget, aip_list_intents.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: list_models, search_apis, discover_agents, aip_execute_with_budget, aip_list_intents · Public sink: chat · Destructive: aip_resolve, aip_execute_with_budget, aip_list_intents

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
